summary: Tested the new admin-customizable check-in form feature (form_heading + typed responses-based check-ins). Backend: rewrote /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py for the new responses[] contract; 18/18 pytest PASS including form_heading persistence, typed custom_fields (text/email/tel/textarea), migration adding contact fields, responses-based POST /api/checkins deriving contractor_name/email server-side, and public+admin GET returning responses[]. Frontend: public check-in shows job.form_heading, renders one type-aware input per custom field, required validation blocks submit with a toast ('Full Name is required.'), valid submit + geolocation succeeds, pin appears on map, pin-count increments. Admin table renders 'Submitted Details' column with label:value pairs (verified: Full Name: TEST_Alice, Email: —, Site Number: B-7, ID Badge Number: —), Coordinates, Job, Timestamp. Admin edit that removed Phone from the seed job propagated to the public page (input-custom-phone absent). ONE HIGH-PRIORITY BUG FOUND: New Job dialog does NOT seed the 3 default fields (Full Name/Email/Phone) and Form Heading is empty — contradicting the spec 'New jobs default to three fields: Full Name (text), Email (email), Phone (tel)'. RCA: emptyJob() in AdminDashboard.js has custom_fields:[] and no form_heading.

minor.issue: Silently accepts payloads WITHOUT a 'responses' field (defaults to []) instead of returning 422. If a legacy client sends old {contractor_name, email, phone, custom_data}, the endpoint returns 200 with empty responses and derived name/email='' — no error surfaced. Consider making responses field required to fail-fast old clients.

ui_bugs.issue: New Job dialog opens with ZERO custom fields and an EMPTY Form Heading. Spec requires it to default to three fields — Full Name (text), Email (email), Phone (tel) — and form_heading='Your Details'. RCA: emptyJob() in /app/frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.js returns { custom_fields: [], ... } and does not set form_heading. Fix: seed emptyJob().custom_fields with [{key:'full_name',label:'Full Name',type:'text',required:true},{key:'email',label:'Email',type:'email',required:true},{key:'phone',label:'Phone',type:'tel',required:true}] and set form_heading:'Your Details'.

testability_issues.issue: Carried over from iteration_2: data-testid="leaflet-map" passed to <MapContainer> is not forwarded to the DOM. Test-only nit; map itself works via .leaflet-container.

action_items: HIGH: Seed emptyJob() in /app/frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.js with 3 default fields (Full Name/Email/Phone) and form_heading='Your Details' so 'New Job' matches the spec.
LOW: Make responses field required (min_items=1?) on CheckInInput in /app/backend/server.py so legacy client shapes get a clear 422 instead of silently creating empty check-ins.
LOW (carry-over from iteration_2): Wrap <MapContainer> in a <div data-testid="leaflet-map"> in /app/frontend/src/components/MapView.js.
LOW (carry-over from iteration_1): Wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch in AdminDashboard.copyLink.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminDashboard.emptyJob(): missing form_heading and the 3 default contact fields — spec violation.
Backend server.py CheckInInput.responses default_factory=list means empty responses are accepted silently. Not a security issue, but weakens the API contract.

server.py create_checkin: name/email derivation uses 'name' in k / 'email' in k substring matching — works but could collide with custom keys like 'company_name' or 'emergency_contact_email'. Consider preferring exact key matches ('full_name', 'name', 'email') first.
AdminDashboard.js is now 435 lines with 4 inline sub-components — still recommend splitting per iteration_1/iteration_2 feedback.

JobDialog.updateField auto-derives 'key' from label — good, but doesn't guarantee uniqueness across fields (two rows labelled 'Full Name' get duplicate keys 'full_name'). Consider suffixing when a collision occurs.

startup migration (server.py lines 371-384) prepends default fields to any job missing form_heading. Verified in test_migration_added_contact_fields (all listed jobs have full_name/email/phone + form_heading).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (rewrote for the new responses-based contract; added form_heading + typed-fields + migration + phone-removal tests)

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (public check-in + admin edit + admin table all pass; New Job dialog defaults are broken)

seed_data_creation: Cleanup done. Deleted TEST_UI_v3_Job and AUTOTEST_Job Renamed via API after testing. TEST_Alice check-in on the seed job (Downtown Tower) and one TEST_User check-in remain in the DB — non-destructive, tied to the seed job the user asked us not to clean up (per 'feel free to leave data').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth is cookie-based httpOnly access_token — pytest fixture sends Cookie header, Playwright uses page.context after login. For public check-in tests: page.context.grant_permissions(['geolocation'], origin=BASE) + set_geolocation({lat:40.7128,lng:-74.006}) BEFORE clicking share-location-btn. When retesting the New Job default-fields bug, click new-job-btn then check custom-field-row count == 3 and their labels are Full Name/Email/Phone with types text/email/tel; also check job-form-heading-input value === 'Your Details'. The [data-testid="leaflet-map"] selector still does NOT resolve — use .leaflet-container instead.
rca_of_the_issue: New Job default-fields bug RCA: In /app/frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.js line ~20, `emptyJob()` returns `{ title:'', description:'', hero_image_url:'', button_label:'Share Location', custom_fields:[], default_map_area:{...}, display_mode:'map', display_image_url:'', display_text:'', active:true }`. It (a) omits `form_heading` entirely (JobDialog defaults it to '' via `editing.form_heading || ''` — placeholder shows 'Your Details' but the input value is empty; only on save does `payload.form_heading = editing.form_heading || 'Your Details'` fall back), and (b) provides an empty custom_fields array so the field-rows list is empty when the admin opens 'New Job'. Fix: `custom_fields: [ {key:'full_name',label:'Full Name',type:'text',required:true}, {key:'email',label:'Email',type:'email',required:true}, {key:'phone',label:'Phone',type:'tel',required:true} ]` and `form_heading: 'Your Details'`.
